title: Gujarat RTE Admissions 2026-27: Apply Online from April 4 to April 17

detail.title: Gujarat RTE Admissions 2026-27: Apply Online from April 4 to April 17
detail.short_info: Gujarat RTE Admission 2026-27 online form is open from April 4 to 17, 2026. Check eligibility, required documents, and step-by-step instructions to fill the RTE Gujarat online form easily.

content.text: The Right to Education (RTE) admission process for Class 1 in Gujarat has officially begun for the 2026-27 academic session. Starting Saturday, April 4, 2026, parents can apply online for their children’s admission under the RTE quota until April 17. This initiative continues to empower families from economically weaker and middle-income groups to access quality education in private schools. This year, around 84,000 RTE seats are available statewide - a slight dip compared to last year’s count of nearly 94,000.
Check Official Website: rte.orpgujarat.com
Apply Link: Gujarat RTE Admissions 2026-27

sections.heading: Gujarat RTE Admissions 2026-27: Apply Online from April 4 to April 17

content.text: The Government of Gujarat has announced the schedule for RTE Gujarat Admission 2026-27 under the Right to Education (RTE) Act. Eligible parents can now apply online to secure free education for their children in private schools. The RTE Gujarat online application form is open from April 4, 2026, and will close at midnight on April 17, 2026.

Under the Right to Education Act, 2009, children from economically weaker sections are entitled to free and compulsory education up to Class 8. Every year, the state invites applications so that eligible students can get admission in private schools where seats are reserved under RTE quota.

rows: RTE Gujarat online form start date
Last date to submit RTE form online

items: The admission is for Class 1 under the RTE quota.
The child must have completed the required age by the official cutoff date; reports indicate the Gujarat cutoff is June 1, 2026 for the 2026-27 cycle.
The family must be eligible under the scheme’s income and category rules, and valid documents must be uploaded during the online application.

sections.heading: Documents Required for RTE Gujarat Admission 2026-27

content.text: While filling the Gujarat RTE form online, parents must upload valid documents for verification. Keep scanned copies ready before starting the form.

items: Click Here: Required documents for filling the form
Click Here: Documents to be uploaded while filling the form

content.text: Required documents include:

items: Proof of date of birth (birth certificate or school record).
Proof of residence (such as Aadhar card, electricity bill, or ration card).
Caste or category certificate, if applicable.
Income certificate issued by a competent authority.
Income tax return document; if not filed, a self-declaration regarding non-income taxable under Income Tax may be submitted.

content.text: Gujarat RTE form online date 2026-27 is from April 4, 2026 to April 17, 2026, and parents can submit the application online through the official portal rte.orpgujarat.com.

sections.heading: Instructions for Filling the Online Form

content.text: To ensure smooth submission, follow the below steps while filling the RTE Gujarat 2026-27 online form:

items: Visit the official RTE Gujarat portal rte.orpgujarat.com.
Read the eligibility criteria and guidelines carefully.
Fill in accurate student and guardian details.
Upload all required documents in the prescribed format.
Submit the form online and download/print the confirmation copy.
Note: The printed form does not need to be submitted anywhere physically.
Make sure all details are correct before final submission inaccurate documents or incorrect information may lead to rejection.

content.text: Click Here: Instructions for filling online form

items: Application window: April 4 to April 17, 2026.
Apply only through the official website.
Keep original documents ready during verification.
Preserve the printed copy for future reference.

content.text: Eligible families are encouraged to apply within the official dates to ensure their children get the benefit of free education under the RTE quota. For more details, visit the Gujarat RTE official portal regularly for updates and announcements.
